sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.content:
HE closes 2025-12-31 with $3.09B of revenue (-4.1% YoY) and $126M of net income versus -$1.42B in the prior period. The period ended with an operating margin of 7.6% and a net margin of 4.1%, which frames how much of the reported scale translated into retained profitability.

Operating cash flow reached $391M (-19.8% YoY), and after $341M of capital spending the business produced $50M of free cash flow (-65.2% YoY). Cash generation exceeded reported earnings, a sign that the period converted accounting profit into liquidity with relatively little friction. Capital spending absorbed 11.1% of revenue, which helps frame how much internal cash flow remained available after reinvestment.

Current liquidity of 1.32x and debt-to-equity of 1.42x point to a balance sheet that is neither clearly stressed nor clearly overcapitalized.

sections.title: Trend Analysis
sections.content:
Across the annual periods shown here, net income provides the clearest recurring pattern, with the latest value at $126M and a compound annual growth rate of 4.3%. The multi-year trend is positive, but the pace is moderate rather than transformative.

The multi-year trajectory has been broadly stable without a strong directional shift. Cash generation remained constructive, with $391M of operating cash flow translating into $50M of free cash flow. Balance-sheet pressure should be read through both leverage (1.42x debt-to-equity) and liquidity (1.32x current ratio), because those measures do not always point to the same conclusion about financial flexibility.

sections.title: Risk Factors
sections.content: No single balance-sheet or earnings issue dominates the reporting period used in this note. The main areas to monitor remain profitability at 7.6% and free cash flow at $50M, because those lines usually determine whether the balance sheet is strengthening or merely holding steady.

sections.title: Analyst Notes
sections.content:
HE's profile does not reduce to a single standout strength or weakness; the more relevant question is how the income statement, cash flow statement, and balance sheet interact. That matters because leverage (1.42x debt-to-equity), liquidity (1.32x current ratio), and free cash flow ($50M) need to be read together rather than in isolation.

This note is anchored to the annual period ending 2025-12-31, with the prior period set at 2024-12-31. Metrics without the required statement support remain blank rather than estimated, so the analysis stays tied to the filing evidence rather than to gap-filled assumptions.

sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.rendered_content:
HE closes 2025-12-31 with $3.09B of revenue (-4.1% YoY) and $126M of net income versus -$1.42B in the prior period. The period ended with an operating margin of 7.6% and a net margin of 4.1%, which frames how much of the reported scale translated into retained profitability.

Operating cash flow reached $391M (-19.8% YoY), and after $341M of capital spending the business produced $50M of free cash flow (-65.2% YoY). Cash generation exceeded reported earnings, a sign that the period converted accounting profit into liquidity with relatively little friction. Capital spending absorbed 11.1% of revenue, which helps frame how much internal cash flow remained available after reinvestment.

Current liquidity of 1.32x and debt-to-equity of 1.42x point to a balance sheet that is neither clearly stressed nor clearly overcapitalized.

sections.title: Trend Analysis
sections.rendered_content:
Across the annual periods shown here, net income provides the clearest recurring pattern, with the latest value at $126M and a compound annual growth rate of 4.3%. The multi-year trend is positive, but the pace is moderate rather than transformative.

The multi-year trajectory has been broadly stable without a strong directional shift. Cash generation remained constructive, with $391M of operating cash flow translating into $50M of free cash flow. Balance-sheet pressure should be read through both leverage (1.42x debt-to-equity) and liquidity (1.32x current ratio), because those measures do not always point to the same conclusion about financial flexibility.

sections.rendered_content: No single balance-sheet or earnings issue dominates the reporting period used in this note. sections.title: Analyst Notes
sections.rendered_content:
HE's profile does not reduce to a single standout strength or weakness; the more relevant question is how the income statement, cash flow statement, and balance sheet interact. That matters because leverage (1.42x debt-to-equity), liquidity (1.32x current ratio), and free cash flow ($50M) need to be read together rather than in isolation.

This note is anchored to the annual period ending 2025-12-31, with the prior period set at 2024-12-31. Metrics without the required statement support remain blank rather than estimated, so the analysis stays tied to the filing evidence rather than to gap-filled assumptions.
